8.reason: Compilation error in C11 and later standards: gets() has been removed from the standard library. Use fgets(s, sizeof(s), stdin); instead.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Compilation error in C11 and later standards: gets() has been removed from the standard library. Use fgets(s, sizeof(s), stdin); instead.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The function 'gets' is unsafe and has been removed from the C standard library since C11; many compilers no longer support it, leading to a compilation error. Use 'fgets' instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Using `gets` is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11 and causes compilation errors with modern compilers. Use `fgets` instead.]

34.reason: [Accessing p[i+1] without ensuring i+1 < lo can cause buffer overread. Also, logic for handling operator precedence and operand parsing is flawed—e.g., it assumes operators always follow numbers directly without considering current position correctly.]
